description_long: This object is a large machete with a broad, slightly curved blade made of metal, showing a matte, weathered finish. The blade is single-edged and tapers to a rounded point, with a visible spine running along its length. The handle is constructed from wood, attached to the tang with two metal rivets, and has a simple, ergonomic shape for a secure grip. There is no visible guard between the blade and handle, giving it a utilitarian appearance. The overall design suggests it is intended for heavy-duty cutting or chopping tasks, commonly used in outdoor or urban environments. No decorative elements or sheath are present, emphasizing its practical function.
description: A large, weathered machete with a broad, slightly curved metal blade and a simple wooden handle secured by rivets. The design is utilitarian, with no guard or ornamentation.
description_view_0: The machete is seen edge-on, showing the thin profile of the blade and the straight alignment of the wooden handle.
description_view_1: This view displays the broad, slightly curved blade and the full wooden handle with visible rivets, highlighting the utilitarian design.
description_view_2: The machete is viewed from the opposite edge, again showing the thinness of the blade and the handle's profile.
description_view_3: A mirrored view of the blade and handle, emphasizing the curvature of the blade and the attachment points of the handle.
attribute_blade_type: single-edged, broad, slightly curved machete blade
attribute_handle_design: straight wooden handle with two metal rivets, ergonomic and unadorned
